items.title: The first sitting US president to attend a NBA game: Jimmy Carter.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
The first sitting US president to attend a NBA game: Jimmy Carter.

He went to a Hawks-Bullets playoff game in 1979 and even took a picture with the Bullets mascot (which was then a dachshund named Tiny).

items.title: June 8, 1986: Larry Bird finishes with 29 PTS/12 AST/11 REB/3 STL in a 114-97 win over the Rockets in Game 6 of the NBA Finals—and the Celtics win their 16th NBA Championship.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 8, 1986: Larry Bird finishes with 29 PTS/12 AST/11 REB/3 STL in a 114-97 win over the Rockets in Game 6 of the NBA Finals—and the Celtics win their 16th NBA Championship.

Also for Boston, Kevin McHale had 29 PTS/10 REB. Akeem Olajuwon scored 19 PTS for the Rockets.

items.title: In every game this postseason, Karl-Anthony Towns has won the minutes he’s played in.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
In every game this postseason, Karl-Anthony Towns has won the minutes he’s played in.

His streak of 16 consecutive games with a positive +/- is the second-longest streak in a single playoff run in the play-by-play era (1996-97), behind Manu Ginobili’s 17-game stretch in 2005.

(via CrazyStats)
#NBA
